title: Spaghetti alla Carbonara Classic

seo.title: Classic Spaghetti Carbonara Recipe: Authentic Italian Pasta
seo.metaDescription: Master the classic Spaghetti Carbonara with our authentic Italian recipe. Learn how to make this creamy, rich pasta dish using traditional ingredients like guanciale and Pecorino Romano. Perfect for a taste of Rome!
longTailKeywords: how to make authentic spaghetti carbonara
seo.h1: Classic Spaghetti Carbonara: Authentic Italian Pasta Recipe
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Spaghetti Carbonara
seo.imageAlt: A close-up of a bowl of classic spaghetti carbonara, garnished with black pepper and Pecorino Romano cheese.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy goodness of authentic classic spaghetti carbonara, a timeless Italian pasta dish.

description: Spaghetti alla Carbonara is a timeless Roman pasta dish celebrated for its rich, creamy texture and savory depth. Made with just a few high-quality ingredients like guanciale, Pecorino Romano, eggs, and black pepper, it embodies the essence of Italian culinary tradition. This classic recipe delivers an unparalleled taste experience, proving that simplicity can lead to extraordinary flavor.

ingredients.notes: cured pork cheek, cut into 1cm cubes

instructions.title: Prepare the Guanciale
instructions.text: Cut the guanciale into small cubes or strips. Place it in a cold, dry skillet over medium-low heat. Cook slowly until crispy and much of its fat has rendered, about 10-15 minutes. Remove the crispy guanciale pieces from the pan and set aside, leaving the rendered fat in the skillet.

instructions.title: Cook the Spaghetti
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of generously salted water to a rolling boil. Add the spaghetti and cook according to package directions until al dente. Reserve about 1 cup (240ml) of the starchy pasta water before draining the spaghetti.

instructions.title: Create the Egg and Cheese Mixture
instructions.text: While the pasta cooks, in a medium bowl, whisk together the egg yolks, the whole egg, and most of the grated Pecorino Romano cheese (reserve a little for garnish). Season generously with freshly ground black pepper. Do not add salt to this mixture yet.

instructions.title: Combine Guanciale and Pasta
instructions.text: Once the spaghetti is al dente, drain it quickly, then immediately transfer it to the skillet with the rendered guanciale fat. Toss the spaghetti with the fat to coat evenly. This helps prevent the eggs from scrambling.

instructions.title: Emulsify with Egg Mixture
instructions.text: Remove the skillet from the heat. Wait about 30 seconds for the pasta to cool slightly. Pour the egg and cheese mixture over the hot spaghetti. Immediately and vigorously toss the pasta, adding a tablespoon or two of the reserved pasta water as needed, until a creamy, glossy sauce forms. The residual heat from the pasta will cook the eggs without scrambling them. The pasta water helps emulsify the sauce.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Add most of the crispy guanciale back into the pasta and toss to combine. If the sauce is too thick, add a little more pasta water; if too thin, add a bit more Pecorino. Taste and adjust seasoning with additional black pepper. Serve immediately, garnished with the remaining crispy guanciale and a sprinkle of Pecorino Romano.

tips: Always use guanciale for authentic Carbonara; pancetta is a common substitute but alters the flavor slightly.
Do not add cream or milk. The creaminess comes from the emulsified egg, cheese, and pasta water.
Serve immediately. Carbonara does not hold well and should be enjoyed fresh for the best texture and flavor.
article: Spaghetti alla Carbonara Classic is a beloved Italian Pasta and Noodles dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
